title: FCoP IPC Envelope
description: Abstraction 3 of 7 — IPC. Per ADR-0015 §abstractions and ADR-0021 §IPC-Surface, this schema covers the four envelope types that share a common frontmatter base: TASK, REPORT, ISSUE, REVIEW. The shared base is intentionally small (protocol/version/sender/type); per-envelope discriminator branches add the type-specific required fields. Root uses `additionalProperties: true` so 0.7.x files with extra unknown frontmatter keys remain valid (per ADR-0003 backward-compat).

roleCode.description: An agent role code. Per agent.schema.json.

priority.description: Priority bucket inherited from 0.7.x convention. P0 = critical, P3 = low. Per existing fcop-spec-v1.0.3.md (preserved verbatim for v1.0).

envelopeBase.description: Shared frontmatter base for all four envelope types (per ADR-0021 §required_frontmatter_base).

protocol.description: Conformance marker. MUST be the literal string 'fcop'.

version.description: FCoP envelope version. v1.0 uses integer 1; will become 2 only on a MAJOR protocol bump per ADR-0003.

date.description: Authoring date. Per 0.7.x convention; not strictly required for v1.0 but MUST validate against isoDate when present.

task.title: TASK envelope
task.description: Per existing 0.7.x spec + ADR-0015. A directive from sender to recipient. Filename: TASK-{date}-{seq}-{from}-to-{to}.md. Per ADR-0003 backward-compat: `subject` is SHOULD-have, not MUST-have — 0.7.x agents commonly carry the subject in the markdown body's first H1 instead of the frontmatter. v1.0 implementations SHOULD write it to frontmatter for indexing.

status.description: Optional explicit status; absent = 'pending'. Per 0.7.x + ADR-0019 (`aborted` added by ADR-0019 §abort recovery action).

ref_task.description: Optional reference to a parent TASK file path.

task_id.description: Per existing 0.7.x. Stable id matching the filename stem; v1.0 keeps it optional in frontmatter (filename is the canonical id).

session_id.description: Per existing 0.7.x. Producing-session identifier for role-occupancy tracking. Optional.

risk_level.description: Per ADR-0024. Operation risk level. low=read-only/no side-effects; medium=normal development (default); high=production/permission changes; irreversible=cannot be undone (drop DB, force-push, over-quota API call). high/irreversible automatically trigger a needs_human review gate (ADR-0025).

report.title: REPORT envelope
report.description: Per existing 0.7.x spec + ADR-0017. The recipient's self-reported status reply to a TASK. Filename: REPORT-{date}-{seq}-{from}-to-{to}.md. Per ADR-0003 backward-compat: 0.7.x REPORTs may use `references: [task_id, ...]` / `related_task` / `related_issues`; v1.0 introduces `ref_task: <path>` as the preferred form. ALL of these are legal v1.0 frontmatter; new files SHOULD prefer `ref_task`. `status` is also SHOULD (some 0.7.x REPORTs encode status only in body).

ref_task.description: v1.0-preferred: path to the TASK file this REPORT replies to.

references.description: 0.7.x form: list of related artefact ids (TASK / ISSUE / others). Kept legal in v1.0 for backward compat per ADR-0003.

related_task.description: 0.7.x alias for ref_task. Kept legal for backward compat; v1.0 implementations SHOULD migrate to `ref_task` on new files.

related_issues.description: 0.7.x alias for ISSUE-style references. Kept legal for backward compat.

report_id.description: 0.7.x stable id matching the filename stem. Optional in v1.0 (filename is canonical).

status.description: Worker self-status. SHOULD be present. `aborted` per ADR-0019 §recovery.

session_id.description: Per existing 0.7.x. Identifies the producing session for role-occupancy tracking. Optional.

issue.title: ISSUE envelope
issue.description: Per existing 0.7.x spec. A blocker or open question raised by sender, not directed at a single recipient. Filename: ISSUE-{date}-{seq}-{from}.md.

ref_task.description: Optional reference to a TASK that triggered the issue.

review.title: REVIEW envelope (v1.1 surface)
review.description: Per ADR-0017 + ADR-0025 + ADR-0026. v1.1 adds needs_human decision value and human_approval sub-structure. Detailed REVIEW schema is in review.schema.json; this stub covers the envelope-level requirements only.

subject_ref.description: Reference to the artefact under review (file path for task/report/code_change, fcop.json roles diff for role_switch). Per ADR-0017.

rationale.description: Body-text rationale for the decision. SHOULD be present for non-`abstained` decisions.

required_changes.description: Per ADR-0017. Required only when decision = `needs_changes`; SHOULD be empty otherwise.
